Acceptance Criteria:
- Honors storage.mode (auto|db|jsonl); auto prefers db when both exist.
- storage.mode=auto uses DB if present; otherwise JSONL if present; errors only when neither exists.
- storage.mode=db uses DB even if JSONL exists; storage.mode=jsonl uses JSONL even if DB exists.

- DB errors (missing path/lock/schema) include agent+alias context.

- Empty JSONL file returns empty list (no error).
- Records are normalized to SessionSummary with required fields (id, agent, alias, title, created_at, updated_at, message_count, storage).

Acceptance Criteria:
- In TUI list, pressing `c` on a codex session opens a clone destination prompt.
- Clone prompt and messaging follow OQO visual priority (RT + LZG secondary).
- Enter confirms the selected destination; Esc cancels the prompt; Ctrl+C exits.
- Hotkeys follow LZG style for the clone prompt (c/Enter/Esc/Ctrl+C).
- Confirming a destination (opencode:<alias>) performs clone and shows a success message with the new session id.
- Canceling the clone prompt leaves the list unchanged and shows no success message.
- Unsupported direction (non-codex source) shows a clear "not supported" message and creates no destination session.
- If clone core errors (unsupported content, validation failure), TUI surfaces the error clearly and creates no destination session.
- After a successful clone, the destination session appears in the list (unless filtered out) and the UI indicates "1 session created".
- TUI is implemented with OpenTUI (opentui).
- Destination options are the enabled opencode aliases from config.
- Unknown destination alias shows a clear error listing available aliases and creates no destination session.
- Source session not found shows a clear error and creates no destination session.
- Clone preserves message order and timestamps in the destination session.
- Clone is read-only on the source session (no source mutations).
- Destination session includes src/dst metadata (src.agent/src.session_id/src.version and dst.agent/dst.session_id/dst.version).
- Destination session id must be new; on collision, clone retries with a new id.
- When OpenCode has both DB and JSONL for the same alias, clone destination uses DB (db preferred).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Adapter reads Codex session storage from adapter-specific config:
  - Uses config `path` (file or directory) resolved relative to the config file directory; if omitted, default is `~/.codex/sessions` (recursive).
  - If `path` is a file, parse a single session; if a directory, recursively read `**/*.jsonl` and ignore other files.
- Codex JSONL mapping:
  - Session id from `session_meta.payload.id` (error if missing).
  - created_at from `session_meta.payload.timestamp`.
  - updated_at is max entry `timestamp` (error if no valid timestamps).
  - title from `session_meta.payload.title` if present and non-empty; else first user message line (response_item payload.role=`user`); fallback to id.
  - message_count is count of response_item entries where payload.role is `user` or `assistant`.
  - storage = other.
- Timestamp parsing is ISO 8601; invalid timestamps return errors with path + record id context.
- Adapter errors include agent+alias context when available and include the resolved path.
- Listing is read-only; no mutations to source storage.
- SessionSummary.id is the codex session id; canonical session key is (agent, alias, session_id).
- Returns normalized SessionSummary with required fields (id, agent, alias, title, created_at, updated_at, message_count, storage).

{"description":"## Problem
The `oas` CLI tool couldn't find sessions when run from a subdirectory because `findProjectId` only looked for exact project worktree matches.

## Root Cause
The `findProjectId` function in `src/adapters/opencode.ts` only checked for exact matches between the current working directory and the project's worktree path.

## Solution
Modified `findProjectId` to walk up the directory tree to find parent projects (similar to git):
1. First tries exact match (preserves existing behavior)
2. If no exact match, walks up directory tree to find closest parent project
3. Returns null if no project found

## Changes Made
- Added `dirname` import from `node:path`
- Rewrote `findProjectId` to support parent directory resolution
- Added 2 new tests to verify the behavior

## Test Results
- ✅ All 100 tests pass (98 existing + 2 new)
- ✅ Works from subdirectories
- ✅ Backward compatible with existing behavior

## Files Modified
- src/adapters/opencode.ts (findProjectId function)
- test/opencode-adapter.test.ts (2 new tests)"}

{"id":"oas-d4a","title":"Epic: Session Reading Interface v1","description":"Time-based session discovery and message reading for oas CLI","notes":"# Intent

Enable users to quickly discover and inspect sessions within a time range, with configurable detail levels and output formats.

## User Context (Verbatim)

User requirements:
- v1: just time range
- text, json, cli first; TUI later phase
- default to hide tools
- judge yourself, aim for neat and ease of use
- TDD, with at LEAST 3 rounds of verification

Additional user needs:
- find previous X sessions, sessions during 4 hours and see what is it doing (usually just need last message or some messages in between, or just reading user messages)
- Cross-session to find WHICH ONE and per-session to find detailed

## Scope

### In Scope (v1)
- Time-based session discovery (--last, --since, --until)
- Message selection (--first, --last, --range, --all, --user-only)
- Tool visibility control (--tools, default hide)
- Output formats (--format text|json)
- Session ID short form support
- CLI integration with existing oas binary

### Out of Scope (v2+)
- Commit-based search
- File-based search
- TUI (interactive interface)
- Cross-session aggregation
- Full-text search (FTS5)

## Approach

Extend existing oas CLI with two new commands:
1. oas sessions - Time-based session discovery
2. oas read - Session detail viewing

Leverage existing OpenCode adapter infrastructure. Add time-based filtering to adapter. Add message selection logic.
